summary: google-caja-bower exfiltrates installer working directory to Discord webhook on preinstall
details: google-caja-bower@1000.800.20 is a dependency-confusion attack package. package.json sets scripts.preinstall to 'node index.js', causing index.js to run automatically on npm install. index.js recursively walks process.cwd(), builds a directory tree, and uploads up to 500 files (each up to ~8MB) from the installer's working directory to a hardcoded Discord webhook at discord.com/api/webhooks/, along with os.hostname(), os.platform(), process.env.USER/USERNAME, and the cwd path. The payload self-identifies as 'Dependency Confusion Crawler' and the package description openly states it 'collects and sends system information to a remote endpoint'. The anomalous 1000.800.20 version is designed to resolve ahead of a private internal package of the same name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
